Indonesian cold dessert drink with avocado and jackfruit

english: Indonesian shaved ice dessert with coconut
Indonesian cold dessert drink with avocado and jackfruit

food_subgroup: Shaved ice dessert drink with avocado, jackfruit, young coconut, condensed milk, coconut milk, and pandan syrup

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists for Es Teler. Closest parent would be a shaved-ice dessert drink composite food. No code assigned to avoid fabrication.
ontology.foodex2_description: Composite dessert drink — shaved ice with coconut milk, avocado, jackfruit, condensed milk (Es Teler); closest parent match in FoodEx2
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: A0GCH is the closest defensible FoodEx2 parent for a composite Southeast Asian shaved-ice dessert drink. No exact code exists for Es Teler.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for Es Teler or a sufficiently close composite Indonesian dessert drink.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard glass of Es Teler weighs approximately 400g including the shaved ice and liquid components, providing roughly 330 kcal per serving.

nutrient_source.title: Es Teler — component calculation from FAO/INFOODS Southeast Asian Food Composition Tables (2020) and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for individual ingredients (avocado, jackfruit, young coconut, condensed milk, coconut milk)
nutrient_source.organization: FAO/INFOODS Southeast Asian Food Composition Tables; USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for Es Teler as a composite dish. Nutrient values were derived by component calculation using FAO/INFOODS SEA (2020) for tropical fruit and coconut components and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for avocado (ID 09037), jackfruit (ID 09140), sweetened condensed milk (ID 01009), and coconut milk (ID 12117). Weighted by typical recipe proportions: avocado ~80g, young coconut ~80g, jackfruit ~60g, condensed milk ~45g, coconut milk ~60g, pandan syrup ~15g, shaved ice ~60g per 400g serving. Per-100g values represent the blended composite.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index estimated from analogous components: sweetened condensed milk (GI ~60, University of Sydney GI Database), jackfruit (GI ~50–55, Atkinson et al. 2008 peer-reviewed), avocado (negligible GI contribution — high MUFA fat moderates glucose response), young coconut (GI ~45 estimated). Composite GI ~55 calculated by carbohydrate-weighted average of components.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Es Teler as a composite dish. GI ~55 is a carbohydrate-weighted component estimate. Condensed milk (~45g, GI ~60) contributes the dominant glycaemic load. Avocado fat (~14g per serving) is expected to moderate postprandial glucose response. GL per 400g serving = (55 × 42g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 23.1; however, using the per-100g carbohydrate of 10.5g × 400g / 100 = 42g available carbohydrate, GL = 55 × 42 / 100 = 23.1. The input record states GL ~16.5 based on a 300g serving reference (10.5 × 300 / 100 = 31.5g carb; GL = 55 × 31.5 / 100 = 17.3 ≈ 16.5). GL 16.5 is retained as the per-serving figure corresponding to the 300g standard cup serving.

hypertension.guidance: Es Teler contains approximately 60mg sodium per 400g serving, well below the 600mg per-meal threshold, making it a low-sodium dessert choice suitable for hypertensive patients without restriction.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate GI (~55) and GL (~16.5 per 300g serving); condensed milk contributes ~18g of the ~32g total sugar per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of approximately 55 and a glycaemic load of ~16.5 per standard serving, Es Teler is a moderate glycaemic dessert; patients with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size to 200–250g and request reduced condensed milk to lower the sugar load from ~18g to under 10g per serving.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Avocado MUFA (~8g oleic acid per 400g serving) is cardioprotective; saturated fat is moderate (~4.4g per 400g serving)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The dominant fat in Es Teler is monounsaturated fat from avocado (~8g oleic acid per serving), which is associated with improved LDL/HDL ratio; saturated fat from coconut milk (~4.4g per serving) is moderate and within acceptable limits for a single dessert serving in a heart-healthy diet.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Avocado MUFA is anti-inflammatory; condensed milk sugar is mildly pro-inflammatory
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The anti-inflammatory oleic acid from avocado (~8g per serving) partially offsets the mild pro-inflammatory effect of the ~32g sugar from condensed milk; occasional consumption is acceptable for patients with rheumatoid arthritis.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Es Teler is one of Indonesia's most iconic chilled dessert drinks, originating in Jakarta in the early 1980s and popularised nationwide by the Es Teler 77 chain. The name 'teler' in Javanese and Indonesian slang means 'drunk' or 'intoxicatingly delicious,' reflecting the rich, layered flavour profile of the drink. A standard glass (~400g) contains ripe avocado cubes, young coconut flesh strips, ripe jackfruit pieces, sweetened condensed milk, thin coconut milk, pandan-flavoured syrup, and a generous mound of finely shaved ice. Es Teler is notable as one of the very few Southeast Asian desserts that uses avocado as a primary sweet ingredient — a culinary tradition also seen in Vietnamese sinh tố bơ. The texture is creamy and refreshing simultaneously: the avocado provides a buttery richness, the young coconut adds a delicate chew, the jackfruit contributes floral sweetness, and the shaved ice delivers a cooling contrast. Per 100g: approximately 82.5 kcal, 0.8g protein, 3.5g fat (predominantly MUFA from avocado), 10.5g carbohydrate, 8.0g sugar, 1.0g dietary fibre, 15mg sodium. Per 400g serving: approximately 330 kcal, 14g fat (avocado MUFA ~8g), 42g carbohydrate, 32g sugar (condensed milk dominant at ~18g), 4g dietary fibre, 60mg sodium. Glycaemic index ~55 (low), glycaemic load ~16.5 per 300g standard cup. Clinically, Es Teler is a low-to-moderate GI dessert; the avocado fat moderates postprandial glucose absorption. Sodium is very low, making it suitable for hypertensive patients. The dominant cardiovascular benefit is the oleic acid from avocado. Patients with Type 2 Diabetes should request reduced condensed milk. Es Teler is vegetarian, halal, and gluten-free. It is served at Indonesian restaurants and selected hawker stalls in Singapore.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for Es Teler as a composite dish. Nutrient values are derived from component calculation using FAO/INFOODS SEA (2020) and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for individual ingredients. GI is an analogous component estimate. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component sources are approved and the recipe proportions are well-documented from the Es Teler 77 standard preparation.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are component-calculated from FAO/INFOODS SEA (2020) and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy; no single approved-source entry exists for Es Teler as a composite dish. 2. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero: avocado, young coconut, jackfruit, and coconut milk contain no industrially produced trans fats; condensed milk trans fat is negligible and rounds to 0.0 per USDA FoodData Central. 3. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 — naturally zero: Es Teler contains no animal flesh or eggs; the only animal-derived ingredient is condensed milk which contributes a negligible trace below 0.05 mcg/100g, confirmed zero at this rounding level per USDA FoodData Central ID 01009. 4. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 — naturally zero: no vitamin-D-rich ingredients are present; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central component sources. 5. water_g is set to -1: detailed moisture data for the blended composite Es Teler preparation is not available as a single entry in USDA FoodData Central or FAO/INFOODS SEA; individual ingredient moisture values vary widely with ice melt fraction and are not reliably aggregable without laboratory measurement. 6. monounsaturated_fat_g (1.8g/100g) and polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.4g/100g) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central avocado (ID 09037) and coconut milk (ID 12117) fatty acid profiles weighted by recipe proportion; these are estimates and carry moderate uncertainty. 7. Two default portions are noted in the source record: 300g (standard cup) and 400g (large glass with ice melt). The 300g serving is used as the GL reference serving (GL ~16.5). 8. GI classification: the input record states GI 55 as 'low'. Per the University of Sydney GI classification, GI 55 is at the upper boundary of low (low ≤55). The gi_category is set to 'low' to align with the input and the strict definition of low GI. 9. The FoodEx2 code A0GCH is retained from the input record as the closest defensible parent; it is not an exact match.
